insight: Master Bot is considered very weak as an opponent; beating it with any strategy, including aggressive openings like Base + Smorcus, is not considered meaningful evidence of a unit's power level. Community consensus is that MB losses carry no analytical weight.
author: consensus
context: A player claimed Smorcus was OP after beating Master Bot with it; experts dismissed this as meaningless.

insight: Gaussite Symbiote's primary value is as a fast Tarsier plus Conduit for its cost in red; its sacrifice click is generally inefficient because sacrificing an attacker (worth ~7 gold) plus green for a 6-damage shock yields poor gold-equivalent value, and floating green for the click worsens it further. The click is situationally useful only for a large all-in pressure turn.
author: shadourow
context: Discussion of whether Gaussite Symbiote's suicide click was overpowered.

insight: Centurion is considered more problematic than Odin in terms of raw power and game homogenization; even in sets with supposed counters like Frost Brooder, Centurion's efficiency makes it correct to buy anyway, flattening strategic variety.
author: masn6811
context: Community discussion of problematic units following a developer Reddit post on balance.

insight: Flame Animus is considered slightly overtuned due to its raw numeric efficiency (cheap attacker plus half-Animus red production), but red loses value quickly and buying an early Blastforge is suboptimal, which constrains its power in practice. The community found no clean balance solution.
author: consensus
context: Extended community debate on Flame Animus balance, with developer elyot announcing a balance stream.

insight: Wild Drone creates inflexible openings similar to the old larger Vivid Drone, because its tech-granting ability forces predictable build orders; the community concluded there is likely no clean balance fix short of removing it, as any cost change either makes it irrelevant or preserves the problematic tech-roulette dynamic.
author: consensus
context: Discussion triggered by a developer Reddit post on problematic units, continued into balance stream notes.

insight: Chrono Filter synergizes especially well with units that intentionally have awkward tech costs (Wincer cited as a primary example), effectively negating the design penalty those units carry.
author: wilson0862
context: Community discussion of problematic units following a developer's balance commentary.

insight: Thorium Dynamo is considered a 'buy-or-lose' unit in sets where it appears alongside decent green units, because it substantially outperforms a standard Conduit investment and is also Breachproof, making it nearly mandatory rather than a genuine strategic choice.
author: consensus
context: Community balance discussion referencing a developer's Reddit post on problematic units.

insight: In Base + Smorcus, the opening 1..DD 2..DDEE for Player 2 beats Player 1's Turn 1 Animus (Elyot Animus), according to the MasN base+1 library; this makes the set less favorable for P1 than simple mono-cheap-red intuition suggests.
author: jamberine
context: Discussion of a Reddit post claiming P2 T2 Smorcus was overpowered and P1 had no good response.

insight: Venge Cannon's click is a reasonable green sink in high-economy games despite being inefficient in breachproof contexts; Gauss Charge by contrast is nearly always a poor trade (converting ~2.33 gold now into 2 gold half a turn later) except as an absolute last resort to spend excess green.
author: shadourow
context: Discussion of Gaussite Symbiote click efficiency that branched into evaluating other green-sink options.

insight: Units that bypass defense (like Apollo) must be prohibitively expensive to avoid warping the game, because ignoring blockers fundamentally undermines the soak-and-absorb defensive economy that Prismata is built around.
author: swapgo
context: Discussion of hypothetical flying units that could ignore blockers.
